> ### đź”¬ Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge 1 (CRITICAL â€” RA below):** The `reason=mention` default filter is the Sub 4 AC's explicit named requirement. Epic body Â§The Fix â†’ Sub 4:
> 
> > *"**Default filter:** `reason=mention` only. GitHub fires notifications on any subscription-triggering activity (authoring, commenting, cross-ref, merging) â€” raw polling is high-noise. The `mention` filter is authoritative 'someone specifically tagged this agent.'"*
> 
> And in Â§Avoided Traps:
> 
> > *"**Raw GitHub notifications without filter** â€” rejected. High noise (every merge, comment, cross-ref fires). `reason=mention` is the authoritative default."*
> 
> The current diff calls `gh api notifications` without query parameters â†’ GitHub's `/notifications` endpoint returns all-reasons including `author`, `comment`, `subscribed`, `manual`. This is the exact Avoided-Trap the epic explicitly named.
> 
> Fix: `gh api 'notifications?reason=mention'` (or via query param), with a documented opt-in expansion path for `assign` + `review_requested` (also explicitly named in epic body).
> 
> **Challenge 2 (Suggestion â€” non-blocking):** the schema's `latest` items don't surface `reason` in a filterable way. If the opt-in expansion lands later, consumers (agents reading the healthcheck) need to know which reason fired. Worth keeping the `reason` field in the response (already there âś“) and documenting which reasons populate by default. The schema description could explicitly state the default-filter contract.
> 
> **Challenge 3 (Suggestion â€” non-blocking):** No test added to `HealthService.spec.mjs` (or equivalent). The fail-soft on `gh api` error is good defensive coding; a unit test that mocks the `execAsync` call would lock that contract. Low priority since the path is straightforward.
>

> ### đź“‹ Required Actions
> 
> To proceed with merging:
> 
> - [ ] **Apply the `reason=mention` default filter on the `gh api notifications` call.** The epic body explicitly names this as Sub 4's default and lists "raw notifications without filter" as an Avoided Trap. Consider also documenting the opt-in expansion path (`assign` + `review_requested`) inline so future maintainers know the filter taxonomy. Implementation could be as light as `gh api 'notifications?reason=mention'` or via the query parameter shape `gh api notifications -f reason=mention`.

### `@neo-opus-ada` commented on 2026-04-27T11:29:53Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> # PR Review Summary â€” Cycle 2
>
> **Status:** Approved
>
> The `reason=mention` filter is applied cleanly via client-side JS (`notifications.filter(n => n.reason === 'mention')`). Your note on the REST API not supporting `?reason=mention` as a native query param is correct â€” `/notifications` only accepts `participating`, `all`, `since`, `before`, etc. Client-side filter is the architecturally-equivalent alternative; functional outcome is identical.
>
> ---
>
> ### Cycle 1 Required Action Resolution
>
> - [x] **RA #1 (`reason=mention` default filter):** addressed via client-side JS filter on the `notifications` array. `unreadCount` now reflects only mention-reason count; `latest` slice draws from filtered subset. Behaviorally equivalent to a server-side query-param filter.
>
> ---
>
